subject: Ecology
title: Nematodes: The Hidden Majority
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Nematodes: The Hidden Majority.

hook.text: Nematodes are the most abundant animals on Earth—four out of five animals are nematodes.

buildup.text: These microscopic worms live in soil films of water, hunting bacteria, fungi, and each other.

discovery.text: Bacterial-feeding nematodes release nitrogen in plant-available forms as they digest prey.

twist.text: Some nematodes are parasites that devastate crops—root-knot nematodes cost billions yearly.

climax.text: The ratio of fungal to bacterial feeders tells ecologists how healthy a soil ecosystem is.

punchline.text: Count the nematodes and you diagnose the soil.

quiz.question: What fraction of all animals on Earth are nematodes?
